title: genista tinctoria subsp. perreymondii
intro: Genista tinctoria subsp. perreymondii, also known as Perreymond's broom or dyer's broom, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Genista tinctoria subsp. perreymondii are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and glycosides, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, supporting cardiovascular health and immune function. Genista tinctoria subsp. perreymondii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and can also be used as a dye, making it a versatile herb in traditional medicine and crafts.
